problem.title: Search Insert Position
problem.description: Given a sorted array of distinct integers and a target value, return the index if the target is found. If not, return the index where it would be if it were inserted in order.
assumptions: Return index if found, else return index where it would be inserted.

example.explanation: 5 is at index 2. If target=2, return 1 (insert position). Binary search.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Binary Search [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Run a standard binary search; if the target is found, return its index. If the loop ends without finding it, the left pointer sits exactly where the target would be inserted to maintain sorted order. The key insight is that the binary search invariant guarantees left always points to the first index whose value is greater than or equal to the target, which is precisely the insertion position.
